tldr: Evenepoel won a 42-km solo breakaway to take Amstel Gold Race, beating defending champion Mattias Skjelmose by one second in what Cyclingnews described as a two-up rematch from the 2025 sprint. Cosnefroy salvaged Decathlon-style third behind. Evenepoel's first major win in Red Bull-Bora kit.

narrative.headline: Evenepoel's 42-km solo, one second clear of last year's winner
narrative.openingMoves: 257.2 km from Maastricht to Berg en Terblijt, the longest Ardennes day. Started at 11:10 CET, finished at 17:12. A long, gradually thinning race that suits the Ardennes set-piece favourites — Evenepoel, Skjelmose, Pogačar (absent), Pidcock (not on the start sheet).
narrative.raceUnfolds: Evenepoel launched what would become the day's race-winning move 42 km from the line — the kind of long-range commitment that has defined his Classics career. Skjelmose, the defending champion, was the only rider who could follow at first, then was distanced.
narrative.decision: The closing kilometres reduced to a two-up rematch from 2025 (when Skjelmose beat Evenepoel in a sprint). One second separated them this year, with Evenepoel's long solo proving the right tactical choice on the Cauberg-and-after climbing sequence.
narrative.finale: Cosnefroy chased home for third — the Decathlon engine that has been at the front of every Ardennes race this spring without the leader-class result. Evenepoel's first big win in Red Bull-Bora kit; Skjelmose's runner-up validates Lidl-Trek's Ardennes pivot.

decisiveMoments.headline: Evenepoel goes solo
decisiveMoments.description: 42 km from the finish, Evenepoel attacked and went alone — committed long-range Classics move. Only Skjelmose could initially follow.

decisiveMoments.headline: Two-up rematch
decisiveMoments.description: Skjelmose held Evenepoel's wheel through the final climbs but couldn't close the last seconds. The 2025 sprint duel inverted: Evenepoel solo by one.

teamStories.verdict: Evenepoel's first major in new colours
teamStories.narrative: After the Ronde podium and the Liège third still to come, Amstel was Evenepoel's first major win in Red Bull-Bora-Hansgrohe kit. The 42-km solo is signature.

teamStories.verdict: Skjelmose runner-up in his title defence
teamStories.narrative: Defending champion finishes one second short. The Ardennes block was always going to be the team's most contested terrain this spring; finishing the first one of three by one second is a clean result.

teamStories.verdict: Cosnefroy at the front without Pogačar
teamStories.narrative: Pogačar skipped Amstel to recover for Flèche and Liège. Cosnefroy carried the team's Ardennes card and took third — the kind of result that justifies UAE's depth.

aftermath.headline: First Red Bull-Bora major
aftermath.body: Evenepoel's spring at Red Bull-Bora-Hansgrohe had been a slow build — Ronde podium, the long Liège breakaway, Amstel win. The 42-km solo is the move Evenepoel has used to win a hundred different races. It worked again.
